rag_rules: Match annex content to the owner scope: type-level content belongs on a component type, while implementation-level content belongs on a component implementation.
If annex content depends on subcomponent paths or connection-level names, keep it in an implementation owner and place it after the referenced declarations.
generic_rules: Make the smallest possible structural edit: add or update only the annex block and preserve existing features, subcomponents, connections, properties, and end identifiers.
Attach the annex only to the intended owner; do not move content across parent, child, or sibling declarations.
Preserve existing AADL declaration order unless scope rules require the annex to follow declarations it depends on.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use the standard AGREE annex wrapper inside the chosen owner body and keep it before that owner's matching end statement.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only references in a type owner.
Do not duplicate, rename, or reorder architectural declarations just to insert an annex block.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE scalar declaration type details and type-mismatch repair
AADL property names or unit tokens as expression variables
